mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already pinned near zero, the discount-rate channel supports equity valuations and S&P 500 returns tend to be positive over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with higher forward returns than unchanged or rising-rate periods.
failure_condition: If triggered easing observations do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than non-easing observations, or if the sign of the average 20d return is negative in a rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals demand deterioration and weaker earnings expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment is rising faster than payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with a 0.3pp three-month UNRATE increase do not have below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in more than 40% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad pickup in retail sales and housing starts indicates improving private demand and revenue prospects, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The mechanism fails when higher demand coincides with sharply tighter monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous above-trend RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially above 50% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a production slump signals a manufacturing rebound and better cyclical earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is falsified if industrial output gains do not precede positive short-horizon equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if months with INDPRO monthly gains above 0.5% do not show positive average 5d SPX returns, or if the effect turns negative in more than half of such months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains low, real financial conditions are easing and equity multiples should expand over the next month. The signal requires disinflation, not just low nominal rates, to be active.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not outperform baseline 20d returns, or if higher inflation with the same policy setting produces equal or better returns.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 following a prior quarter of growth signals aggregate demand weakening and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if GDP contraction quarters are not followed by equity weakness.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on the latest quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with negative GDPC1 growth do not have negative average 60d SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS indicates labor-market stress, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through weaker income and earnings expectations. Either series alone is insufficient; the joint deterioration must be present.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if joint UNRATE-up/PAYEMS-down episodes do not show negative average 20d SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening phase, rising INDPRO together with increasing HOUST signals improving real activity and supply normalization, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if production and housing rise during tightening-driven slowdowns without positive equity follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if paired gains in INDPRO and HOUST do not lead to positive average 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional effect disappears in a rolling 2-year window.
